reasoning:
The resolution strictly requires the US government to publicly accuse China of blockading at least one of Taiwan's seaports without Taipei's consent before July 1, 2025. It is early May 2025, leaving less than two months for this to happen. A Chinese blockade of an internationally recognized or even offshore Taiwanese seaport would represent an extreme escalation, precipitating an international crisis that falls just short of or sparks an active war. Extensive searching across general news and official US State Department avenues shows no such accusation has been made to date. There have only been simulations of blockades in military exercises around April 2025, but no initiation of an actual blockade of a port, which is required for this resolution.

Since the base rate of this event is practically 0% historically, and there is no indication that an actual blockade is ongoing or imminent in the next ~8 weeks, the probability remains exceedingly low. Therefore, the minimum allowed probability of 2% is the most justifiable estimate.
